_protocol: Phase 3.5-PRAGMATIC: 5+red+5 fetches with adversarial gap review COMPLETED

gaps_identified: Frontier-inference provider concentration: OpenAI ($25B) + Anthropic ($44B) dominate but distribution across hyperscalers post-Apr 27
Reference class: cloud top-3 share 43% (2014) → 56% (2018) → 68% (2026) — plateau in H2-H3 boundary
Neoclouds (CoreWeave $5.13B, Together $300M, Lambda $500M, Fireworks) growing but still small vs $268B top-3 cloud
FTC/DOJ antitrust scrutiny exists but Trump admin pro-consolidation; major hyperscaler M&A unlikely blocked
OpenAI selling on AWS Bedrock/Vertex post-exclusivity = revenue distribution across cloud channels

reference_class.definition: Tech-platform consolidation cycles (cloud 2010-2020, mobile OS 2008-2015, browsers 2003-2010, public clouds 2010-2020)

analog_events.name: Browsers 2003-2010
analog_events.outcome: Multiple major resets; never settled top-3 above 80%

topic_priors.rationale: Round 2 found: (a) Cloud top-3 plateaued ~65-70% — 4yr projection from 68% lands H2-H3 boundary. (b) OpenAI distribution across hyperscalers (post-Apr 27) reduces channel concentration even as provider concentration holds. (c) Anthropic $44B ARR growing fast but on multiple clouds. (d) Neoclouds adding fragmentation pressure (~$15-20B combined). Net: H1 down 2pp (less fragmentation than open-weights signal alone suggested), H2 down 2pp (concentration edges higher), H3 up 4pp (oligopoly threshold trajectory most aligned with cloud-history reference class), H4 stays at 0.10 (extreme concentration unlikely with open weights at 67% enterprise + neoclouds growing).

extracted._implication: Frontier-API providers concentrate via 2 labs (OpenAI + Anthropic) but distribution across 3 hyperscalers post-Apr 27 deal; concentration on PROVIDER axis stays high but cloud-channel concentration may dilute

extracted._pattern: Top-3 cloud share grew ~5pp/year over 2014-2018, plateaued ~65-70% by 2026
extracted._implication: Mature platform plateau is in H2-H3 boundary range (65-70%); 4-year-out projection from 68% suggests 70-75% range — H3 trajectory most likely
Neoclouds growing fast but still small (~$15-20B combined vs $268B top-3 cloud); top-3 cloud holds primary inference channel; some fragmentation pressure
Antitrust scrutiny exists but Trump admin posture is pro-consolidation; major hyperscaler AI M&A unlikely to be blocked; supports H3/H4 oligopoly path

Distribution shifting — OpenAI now sells across multiple hyperscalers, distributing the largest single revenue source. Reduces concentration on cloud channel even as provider concentration (OpenAI+Anthropic) holds
